response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software engineering maturity with 20 years of experience and clear systems-thinking skills, his profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. He has minimal recent C++ experience (last significant use ~6 years ago), primarily recent work in TypeScript/Node.js, and zero background in industrial automation, IoT, or manufacturing domains. The role requires current C++ expertise and IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity knowledge; this candidate would face a significant ramp-up period on primary competencies.
strengths: 20+ years software experience with proven systems architecture expertise (GISCollective event-driven platform, microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
Strong testing and quality culture (TDD proponent, created Fluent-Asserts and Trial test runner libraries)
Demonstrated ability to learn diverse technology stacks and mentor teams across multiple languages and domains
concerns: C++ proficiency is stale—last significant use ~6 years ago at National Instruments; primary focus since 2015 is DLang, Node.js, and TypeScript
No background in industrial automation, IoT, manufacturing, or industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA)
Missing industrial cybersecurity expertise—no mention of IEC 62443, SDL practices, or secure development lifecycle in manufacturing contexts
